title: Lucid (LCID) Q1 2026 Earnings Preview: What Wall Street Forecasts Ahead of Tuesday's Report
text: Revenue was expected to be approximately $370 million, representing a 57% year-over-year increase. EPS loss was expected to be $2.35 per share, slightly improved from the $2.40 loss in Q1 2025. Stock was trading near $6.53, down 38% year-to-date, with analyst price target of $13.13 implying over 100% upside potential. The consensus rating among 10 Wall Street analysts was hold, consisting of 7 hold ratings, 2 sell ratings, and 1 buy rating. Options markets anticipated a significant 13.30% price swing following the earnings release, well above the typical 5.24% post-earnings move. Critical focus areas included the Gravity SUV experiencing a 29-day delivery suspension during the quarter stemming from a supplier-related problem, with investors seeking clarity on resolution and full-year production guidance of 25,000-27,000 vehicles. Uber expanded its vehicle order commitment to a minimum of 35,000 units for robotaxi deployment and now held an 11.52% ownership stake in Lucid following a $200 million investment. Lucid had missed earnings in six of the last nine quarters.
